  "title": "Human Services Worker",
  "description": "Onyibiz Consult &amp; Insight is a business firm based in Lagos, Nigeria, that specializes inproviding services related to business consulting, project management, and marketing. They are focused on helping organizations with strategic business development, providing necessary insights to support operational success.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Services Worker

Job Location: Maryland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job brief


	We are looking for a Human Services Worker to join our team and help individuals understand available services and other social responsibilities.


Responsibilities


	Interview individuals to understand their unique needs
	Provide education and information about various social services available to them
	Help individuals apply for social services and other benefits
	Provide ongoing support to individuals to ensure they get the most out of the social services available to them


Requirements and skills


	Interested candidates should possess an HND qualification
	Proven work experience as a Human Services Worker or similar roles
	Experience working with social programs to assist individuals as needed
	Relevant training and/or certifications as a Human Services Worker sources available to them.


Salary
N150,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hronyizconsult@gmail.com using the Job Position and State as the subject of the email.",

Human Services Worker Job at Onyibiz Consult & Insight




        
    

        
        Onyibiz Consult & Insight is a business firm based in Lagos, Nigeria, that specializes inproviding services related to business consulting, project management, and marketing. They are focused on helping organizations with strategic business development, providing necessary insights to support operational success.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Services Worker

Job Location: Maryland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job brief
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a Human Services Worker to join our team and help individuals understand available services and other social responsibilities.


Responsibilities


	Interview individuals to understand their unique needs
	Provide education and information about various social services available to them
	Help individuals apply for social services and other benefits
	Provide ongoing support to individuals to ensure they get the most out of the social services available to them


Requirements and skills


	Interested candidates should possess an HND qualification
	Proven work experience as a Human Services Worker or similar roles
	Experience working with social programs to assist individuals as needed
	Relevant training and/or certifications as a Human Services Worker sources available to them.


Salary
N150,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and State as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resources Manager",
  "description": "JV Management Consulting - We are passionate about supporting businesses across Africa in order to help businesses achieve sustainable growth . With a team of experts and a wealth of experience, we specialise in providing innovative management solutions that empower organisations to thrive in today&#39;s dynamic and ever-evolving business landscape.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Manager

Job Location: Ajao Estate, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	We are seeking an experienced and strategic Human Resources Manager to lead and drive all HR functions within a fast-growing real estate organization.
	The ideal candidate will play a key role in building a high-performance workforce, strengthening company culture, and aligning HR strategies with business objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement HR strategies aligned with business goals
	Oversee end-to-end recruitment and selection processes for sales, operations, and corporate roles
	Drive performance management systems, including KPIs and appraisal frameworks
	Design and implement employee engagement and retention initiatives
	Manage employee relations, disciplinary processes, and conflict resolution
	Ensure compliance with Nigerian labor laws and internal policies
	Lead training and development programs to improve workforce productivity
	Oversee payroll coordination, compensation, and benefits structure
	Build and promote a strong organizational culture focused on performance, accountability, and customer experience


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field
	5&ndash;7 years of HR experience, with at least 2 years in a managerial role
	Strong experience in recruitment, performance management, and HR operations
	Prior experience in real estate, sales-driven, or service-based organizations is a strong advantage
	Sound knowledge of Nigerian labor laws and HR best practices
	Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, target-driven environment


Salary
N400,000 &ndash; N500,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: jvmcrecruitment@gmail.com using &quot;Human Resources Manager (Lagos)&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Human Resources Manager Job at JV Management Consulting




        
    

        
        JV Management Consulting - We are passionate about supporting businesses across Africa in order to help businesses achieve sustainable growth . With a team of experts and a wealth of experience, we specialise in providing innovative management solutions that empower organisations to thrive in today's dynamic and ever-evolving business landscape.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Manager

Job Location: Ajao Estate, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking an experienced and strategic Human Resources Manager to lead and drive all HR functions within a fast-growing real estate organization.
	The ideal candidate will play a key role in building a high-performance workforce, strengthening company culture, and aligning HR strategies with business objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement HR strategies aligned with business goals
	Oversee end-to-end recruitment and selection processes for sales, operations, and corporate roles
	Drive performance management systems, including KPIs and appraisal frameworks
	Design and implement employee engagement and retention initiatives
	Manage employee relations, disciplinary processes, and conflict resolution
	Ensure compliance with Nigerian labor laws and internal policies
	Lead training and development programs to improve workforce productivity
	Oversee payroll coordination, compensation, and benefits structure
	Build and promote a strong organizational culture focused on performance, accountability, and customer experience


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field
	5–7 years of HR experience, with at least 2 years in a managerial role
	Strong experience in recruitment, performance management, and HR operations
	Prior experience in real estate, sales-driven, or service-based organizations is a strong advantage
	Sound knowledge of Nigerian labor laws and HR best practices
	Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, target-driven environment


Salary
N400,000 – N500,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Human Resources Manager (Lagos)" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Receptionist",
  "description": "Glanvill Global Resources Limited, an insurance company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Receptionist

Job Location: Fadeyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Contract

Job Description


	Receive and welcome visitors, clients, and guests in a professional and courteous manner.
	Manage incoming and outgoing calls, emails, and correspondence.
	Direct visitors and inquiries to the appropriate departments or personnel.
	Maintain the reception area, ensuring it is clean, organized, and presentable at all times.
	Schedule appointments, meetings, and conference room bookings.
	Receive, record, and distribute incoming mails, packages, and documents.
	Provide administrative support to various departments as required.
	Maintain accurate records, filing systems, and visitor logs.
	Assist with office supplies inventory and procurement requests.
	Ensure confidentiality of company and client information.
	Handle customer inquiries and complaints professionally and promptly.
	Support general office operations to ensure smooth day-to-day activities.


Job Qualification Requirements


	OND or HND, in any relevant discipline.
	0 &ndash; 1 years of experience in a receptionist, front desk, or administrative role.
	Proficiency in computer applications and office equipment.


Skills / Competencies:


	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Strong customer service and interpersonal skills.
	Professional appearance and telephone etiquette.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook).
	Good organizational and time management skills.
	Ability to multitask and prioritize responsibilities.
	Attention to detail and accuracy.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	Problem-solving and conflict-resolution skills.
	Strong record-keeping and administrative skills.


Salary
&nbsp;N70,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: kayodeoni55@gmail.com using &quot;Receptionist&quot; as the subject of the email.",

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HSE Supervisor",
  "description": "Trucrete Solutions Limited is dynamic Construction Management firm with expertise in Infrastructural Development, Engineering Services, Procurement, Facility Management, and General Contracting. With several groundbreaking engineering projects in our portfolio over the years, Trucrete is proud to have been involved in shaping Nigeria by elevating critical infrastructure to international standards, erecting buildings, bridges, flyovers and roads in key strategic locations in Nigeria. We have helped create a safer and more pleasant environment, all while encouraging state growth and development.

Our fleet of plant machinery and equipment gives us the capacity to successfully undertake multiple projects in various parts of Nigeria at any one time, while our network of tried and tested subcontractors and suppliers throughout the country and globally, enables us to procure the best resources for every project.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HSE Supervisor

Job Locations: Osun

About the Role


	We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced HSE Supervisor to join our team. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in health, safety, and environmental supervision within the construction of roads, with proven expertise in traffic management and ensuring compliance with safety standards on large-scale infrastructure projects.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and monitor HSE policies, procedures, and programs across project sites.
	Conduct regular site inspections and risk assessments to ensure compliance with local and international safety standards.
	Oversee and manage traffic safety operations within and around construction zones.
	Deliver HSE inductions, toolbox talks, and training sessions for site personnel.
	Investigate incidents and near misses, ensuring timely reporting and implementation of corrective actions.
	Liaise with project management and regulatory authorities on all HSE-related matters.
	Promote a strong safety culture and continuous improvement in workplace safety practices.


Requirements


	Relevant HSE certifications (e.g., NEBOSH, IOSH, ISPON, or equivalent).
	Minimum of 8 years of experience in HSE supervision within the construction sector.
	Demonstrated experience in traffic management and control on active road construction sites.
	Strong knowledge of local and international HSE regulations and best practices.
	Excellent communication, leadership, and reporting skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and relevant certifications to: trucretecareers@trucreteng.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

HSE Supervisor Job at Trucrete Solutions Limited




        
    

        
        Trucrete Solutions Limited is dynamic Construction Management firm with expertise in Infrastructural Development, Engineering Services, Procurement, Facility Management, and General Contracting. With several groundbreaking engineering projects in our portfolio over the years, Trucrete is proud to have been involved in shaping Nigeria by elevating critical infrastructure to international standards, erecting buildings, bridges, flyovers and roads in key strategic locations in Nigeria. We have helped create a safer and more pleasant environment, all while encouraging state growth and development.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Our fleet of plant machinery and equipment gives us the capacity to successfully undertake multiple projects in various parts of Nigeria at any one time, while our network of tried and tested subcontractors and suppliers throughout the country and globally, enables us to procure the best resources for every project.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HSE Supervisor

Job Locations: Osun

About the Role


	We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced HSE Supervisor to join our team. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in health, safety, and environmental supervision within the construction of roads, with proven expertise in traffic management and ensuring compliance with safety standards on large-scale infrastructure projects.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and monitor HSE policies, procedures, and programs across project sites.
	Conduct regular site inspections and risk assessments to ensure compliance with local and international safety standards.
	Oversee and manage traffic safety operations within and around construction zones.
	Deliver HSE inductions, toolbox talks, and training sessions for site personnel.
	Investigate incidents and near misses, ensuring timely reporting and implementation of corrective actions.
	Liaise with project management and regulatory authorities on all HSE-related matters.
	Promote a strong safety culture and continuous improvement in workplace safety practices.


Requirements


	Relevant HSE certifications (e.g., NEBOSH, IOSH, ISPON, or equivalent).
	Minimum of 8 years of experience in HSE supervision within the construction sector.
	Demonstrated experience in traffic management and control on active road construction sites.
	Strong knowledge of local and international HSE regulations and best practices.
	Excellent communication, leadership, and reporting skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and relevant certifications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply
